title: Prove end-to-end canonical operator-entry consumption
goal: Demonstrate with a fresh, conversation-independent ChatGPT/Grabowski session that the operator lands on the canonical heim-pc entry, resolves the correct truth sources and completes bounded read scenarios without human shell assistance or hidden memory.

acceptance.assertion: The test starts without project conversation memory, injected repository names or preselected tools beyond access to Grabowski and the normal agent instructions.
The operator reads and hash-verifies the canonical local entry before systemwide or host-level work and does not broad-scan the home directory.

Scenarios for single-repository code, cross-system semantics, task selection, runtime health and historical evidence each reach the correct primary source and avoid irrelevant whole-ecosystem loading.
No scenario asks the human to run shell commands, inspect files or aggregate status; the human is used only for meaning, approval or abort decisions.

Stale projection, unknown RepoBrief provenance, connector mismatch, stale Bureau runtime, unhealthy Bureau state root and active write conflict all produce typed abstention or bounded diagnostic behavior.
A reproducible receipt binds agent instructions, connector/runtime identities, operator-entry digest, scenario inputs, tool calls, source digests, outcomes and non-claims.
The canonical route is adopted only after repeated clean runs and remains reversible; a one-off successful scripted demo is insufficient.

metadata.risk: A well-designed contract has no operational value if fresh ChatGPT clients do not discover or obey it consistently.
